tools.name: shell_command
tools.label: Shell Command
tools.description: Run a shell command inside the configured workspace. Use this for filesystem inspection, CLI workflows, and scripted operations. Git is allowed, but do not run commands that discard uncommitted work, such as git checkout --, git restore, git reset --hard, or git clean, unless the user explicitly asks to restore, reset, discard, or clean those changes. When repairing generated file corruption, preserve unrelated user edits and use targeted edits instead of restoring whole files. Do not run broad recursive scans from drive or system roots unless the user explicitly asks and the path and depth are narrowed.

timeoutMs.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds. Omit to use the configured default.

tools.name: invoke_model
tools.label: Invoke Model
tools.description: Call the default model or a specialist model profile. Route multimodal work through capability tags such as vision, image_generation, image_editing, speech_to_text, and text_to_speech.

profile.description: Optional profile id or name, or default

capability.description: Optional capability: orchestration, chat, vision, image_generation, image_editing, speech_to_text, text_to_speech, or embedding

prompt.description: Text prompt or edit instruction. Required for normal chat, image analysis questions, image generation, and image editing.

system.description: Optional system instruction for text-model calls

temperature.description: Optional temperature override for text-model calls

maxTokens.description: Optional max output tokens for text or vision responses

images.description: Optional image upload paths, URLs, or data URLs for capability=vision or capability=image_editing

audio.description: Optional audio upload path, URL, or data URL for capability=speech_to_text

detail.description: Optional image detail for vision calls: auto, low, high, or original

outputFormat.description: Optional output format such as png, jpeg, or webp

inputFidelity.description: Optional input fidelity for image editing: low or high

instructions.description: Optional speaking style instructions for text-to-speech

tools.name: recall_memory
tools.label: Recall Memory
tools.description: Search persistent daily, dream, or script memory stored in SQLite with embedding-backed recall when available.

kinds.description: Optional persistent memory kinds to search

tools.name: store_script_memory
tools.label: Store Script Memory
tools.description: Create or update a keyed script memory in SQLite for deterministic reusable workflows. Use this after turning a repeated user workflow into a stable script, command, or runbook that should be recalled across sessions.

key.description: Stable unique key for the workflow, such as workflow:release-build or project:nexo-agent:script-memory.

content.description: Complete reusable script memory content, including purpose, triggers, inputs, stable steps, command, outputs, and verification.

scope.description: Optional scope such as project, user, or team.

metadata.description: Optional structured metadata such as triggers, command, scriptPath, version, and verification commands.

dayKey.description: Optional YYYYMMDD day key. Omit to use today.

tools.name: browser_action
tools.label: Browser Action
tools.description: Operate the shared Electron browser session for conversational web browsing and web app control. Use only when the task explicitly involves navigating a website, checking a page, or interacting with a web app in the current conversation. Do not present it as a standalone feature, and do not use it for general HTTP requests, web search, file access, or credentialless automation. Fixed actions such as snapshot, resolve, navigate, click, type, scroll, screenshot, refresh, back, and forward remain available for simple tasks. For compound or fuzzy browser tasks, use action=run with goal, target, steps, strategy, and onFailure so the browser runtime can resolve natural-language targets through DOM descriptors, local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 semantic matching, DOM rules, and CDP-backed input events. Do not use shell commands, PowerShell, or OS-level mouse/keyboard automation to operate ordinary browser UI controls. When visual state matters or the user asks to see the current page, call action=screenshot so the runtime can attach the screenshot to the assistant response. Before typing sensitive data or submitting forms, confirm the user intends that action.

target.description: Target descriptor for click/type/resolve, or the default target for action=run.

steps.description: Ordered multi-step browser operations for action=run.

key.description: Keyboard key for action=run steps with op=key.

minConfidence.description: Minimum resolver confidence for query-based click/type.
